holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Nifty50 Shariah index excludes companies operating in interest-bearing finance, alcohol, tobacco, pork, media and weapons sectors. Infosys is the largest constituent as banking/finance stocks are excluded. Shariah-screened index has a strong IT and pharma tilt.

holdings.name: Tata Consultancy Services
holdings.description: TCS is 2nd largest in Shariah index absent banks. IT companies natural fit for Islamic finance compliance — pure technology services with no interest income. TCS generates 100% of revenue from technology services.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: 3rd largest after IT sector receives overweight in Shariah vs conventional index. Halal-compliant technology business model — revenues from outsourcing, products and engineering services, zero interest income.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Pharma companies are Shariah-eligible. Sun Pharma is largest pharma company, gains significant weight absent HDFC Bank (~14% in cap-weight) and other banks. Pharmaceutical manufacturing is halal-permissible activity.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: Engineering and construction — Shariah-eligible. L&T's heavy engineering and EPC operations are permissible under Islamic finance principles. Elevated weight vs cap-weight index due to bank exclusion redistributing capital.

holdings.name: Maruti Suzuki
holdings.description: Auto manufacturing is Shariah-compliant. Maruti gains outsized weight in Shariah index. Muslim-majority countries (Gulf, Indonesia, Malaysia) are growing export markets for Maruti — strategic fit between index and business geography.
